chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI C.P. No. D-1643 of 2026 [Daniel Masih V. Province of Sindh and others] Date Order with signature of Judge(s) Before: Mr. Justice Adnan-ul-Karim Memon Mr. Justice Zulfiqar Ali Sangi Date of hearing and Order: 28.04.2026 Mr. Shahid Hussain, Advocate for the Petitioner. Mr. Muhammad Ibrahim Soomro, Advocate for Respondents 4 & 5. Mr. Asad Ahmed, Advocate for the Respondent/KMC. ********** O R D E R Adnan-ul-Karim Memon, J. – The petitioner, Daniel Masih, has invoked the constitutional jurisdiction of this Court under Article 199 of the Constitution of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan, 1973, seeking a declaration that he is lawfully entitled to receive the retirement benefits, including gratuity, from the respondents. He further prayed for a direction to the respondents, being a statutory body functioning under the provincial government, to act strictly in accordance with the law and the constitutional framework, and to release the said benefits without any further delay, asserting that recovery of such dues constitutes his fundamental right. 2. During the course of proceedings, learned counsel appearing on behalf of TMC Landhi submitted that the case pertaining to the petitioner’s deceased wife for pensionary benefits would be forwarded to KMC for necessary processing within one week. It was further stated that, upon such transmission, the requisite process would be completed by the KMC and the pensionary benefits would be released within a period of two weeks. This request is found to be reasonable and is accordingly accepted. 3. In view of the above statement and undertaking, the instant petition is disposed of, along with all pending applications, in the aforesaid terms.

This request is found to be reasonable and is accordingly accepted. 3. In view of the above statement and undertaking, the instant petition is disposed of, along with all pending applications, in the aforesaid terms. JUDGE JUDGE Ayaz Gul
